description: Interactive onboarding for new VS Code contributors.
walkthrough.title: Contribute to VS Code
walkthrough.description: Follow the contributor journey from choosing an issue to opening a pull request.
walkthrough.contributing.title: Open the local contributor guide
walkthrough.contributing.description:
Start with the repository's CONTRIBUTING.md to see the full contributor journey in this workspace.
[Open CONTRIBUTING.md](command:contributor-onboarding.openContributing)
walkthrough.chooseWork.title: Choose the right work
walkthrough.chooseWork.description:
Start with a suitable issue, check for duplicates, and comment when you decide to work on it.
[Browse good first issues](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22good%20first%20issue%22)
walkthrough.discussFirst.title: Know when to discuss first
walkthrough.discussFirst.description:
Some changes need alignment before implementation, especially feature requests, UX changes, architecture changes, and API proposals.
[Read issue triaging guidance](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Issues-Triaging)
walkthrough.architecture.title: Map the architecture
walkthrough.architecture.description:
Understand the layer that owns the behavior before changing code.
[Open Source Code Organization](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/source-code-organization)
walkthrough.localSetup.title: Set up and reproduce locally
walkthrough.localSetup.description:
Build Code - OSS, run the development version, and reproduce the issue before editing.
[Open setup guide](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute)
walkthrough.aiWorkflow.title: Use AI as a contributor assistant
walkthrough.aiWorkflow.description:
Use Copilot Chat or another assistant to inspect context, but keep the contributor responsible for architecture, validation, and review readiness.
[Open Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%7B%22mode%22%3A%22ask%22%7D)
walkthrough.validateAndPr.title: Validate and open the pull request
walkthrough.validateAndPr.description:
Run the checks that match the changed area, describe how to test the change, and follow review and iteration planning.
[Read pull request guidance](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute#pull-requests)
